headline: Building a ten-foot interface for KDE Plasma
lede: Plasma Bigscreen brings the desktop environment to the living room experience. This project adapts standard interface elements into a remote-friendly, large-scale navigation system.

headline: Standardizing UUID support in Go
lede: The Go language maintainers are moving to integrate native UUID support directly into the standard library. Developers can finally shed third-party dependencies for unique identifier generation.

headline: Proving humanity through browser-based CSS
lede: In an era of automated interactions, this experiment uses stylesheet rendering to distinguish human intent from script-based noise. It is a whimsical look at the intersection of aesthetic design and verification.

headline: Philanthropic intervention in rare disease pathways
lede: Private capital is increasingly funding the search for treatments where commercial pipelines have stalled. This investigation examines whether concentrated wealth can genuinely alter the trajectory of neurodegenerative conditions.

headline: Searching for patterns in the noise
lede: Identifying underlying structures in complex data requires more than just statistical significance. This reflection explores how cognitive biases and pattern-seeking behaviors shape our perception of modern events.

headline: Why LLMs fail at complex coding tasks
lede: To move beyond boilerplate generation, users must shift from prompts to precise acceptance criteria. Without rigid constraints, the output remains prone to subtle, dangerous errors.
pullquote: The quality of generated code is entirely dependent on the rigor of the user's defined requirements

headline: Hardening Firefox with Anthropic red team insights
lede: Security researchers are deploying LLM-assisted red teaming to identify critical vulnerabilities in the browser architecture. These findings underscore a proactive approach to neutralizing exploit surfaces.

headline: Galileo's forgotten annotations found in ancient text
lede: A rediscovered copy of a sixteenth-century astronomy volume reveals handwritten margin notes from Galileo himself. These markings provide a rare glimpse into the observational process of a foundational scientist.

headline: Querying massive vector datasets
lede: Scaling retrieval systems to handle planetary-scale data remains a significant engineering hurdle. We examine the performance trade-offs required to query across billions of dimensions efficiently.

headline: Implicit string conversions destroying SQL indexes
lede: In the shadow of C# to SQL mapping, implicit type conversion can silently degrade database performance. This deep dive uncovers why Dapper requires explicit handling to avoid index scans.
